source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, the vertices of $\triangle ABC$ are all on the vertices of small squares with equal side lengths. What is the value of $\cos\angle BAC$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\[ \textbf{Solution:} \text{As shown in the figure, construct } CD\perp AB \text{ at point } D, \]
\[ \text{Given that, } S_{\triangle ABC}=\frac{1}{2}\times 2\times 2=2, \quad AB=\sqrt{2^{2}+4^{2}}=2\sqrt{5}, \quad AC=2\sqrt{2}, \]
\[ \therefore S_{\triangle ABC}=\frac{1}{2}AB\cdot CD=2, \quad CD=\frac{2S_{\triangle ABC}}{AB}=\frac{4}{2\sqrt{5}}=\frac{2\sqrt{5}}{5}, \]
\[ \text{In } \triangle ADC, \quad AD=\sqrt{AC^{2}-CD^{2}}=\frac{6\sqrt{5}}{5}, \]
\[ \therefore \cos\angle BAC=\frac{AD}{AC}=\frac{\frac{6\sqrt{5}}{5}}{2\sqrt{2}}=\boxed{\frac{3\sqrt{10}}{10}}. \]

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "As shown in the figure"
the needed triangle side/angle relationships come from vertex positions on a square grid shown in the image
multi_step: solving requires extracting geometric lengths or coordinates from the grid, then combining them to compute cos(angle BAC)
answer_text shows multiple derived quantities (area, side lengths, altitude, projection) before reaching the final cosine value
this is not a direct lookup or single-step arithmetic question
verifiability: the target answer is a single closed-form value, \"3sqrt(10)/10\", which is easy to compare against
the reasoning can be checked from geometric relations once the diagram is interpreted
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present
